content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 13, 1986

Joanne Zumwalt
Willie Hausey & Associates, Inc.
1127 11th Street, #321
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: FPPC No. A-86-170

Dear Ms. Zumwalt:

Thank you for your letter dated May 15, 1986, in which you
asked for clarification of the lobbying disclosure provisions as
they apply to Mr. Willie Hausey. You stated the following:

Mr. Willie Hausey is an "in-house lobbyist' employed
full-time by Watts Health Foundation. Mr. Hausey
formed a lobbying firm of which he is president, and
contracts with one lobbyist to lobby for the clients
of this firm.

Because Mr. Hausey is owner of a lobbying firm, Mr. Hausey
must register and file reports as a "lobbying firm," and not as an
"in-house lobbyist." In addition, Watts Health Foundation should
terminate its lobbyist employer registration because it employs a
lobbying firm, and entities which employ a lobbying firm are not
required to register.

Mr. Hausey may, if he wishes, explain on his registration
statement and on his quarterly reports that he is a full-time
employee of Watts Health Foundation and that he does not
personally provide any lobbying services for the clients who
contract with Willie Hausey & Associates.

Mr. Hausey's registration as a lobbying firm should be
effective as of the date of this letter. It is not necessary to
amend the previous quarterly reports filed by Watts Health
Foundation or the previous quarterly reports filed by Willie
Hausey & Associates.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Joanne Zumwalt
June 13, 1986
Page 2


     Enclosed are the informational materials and forms necessary
for filing the amendments.  Please call Colleen McGee at 322-5662
before completing the amendments.  Mrs. McGee will explain in
detail how to prepare the amendments.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Division Chief, Technical
                                        Assistance & Analysis Division

JP:kt

cc:  Secretary of State
     Political Reform Division

Willie Hausey & Associates, Inc.
1127 11th Street, #321, Sacramento, CA 95814 • 443-6451

Willie Hausey
President

Joanna Zumalt
Associate

MAY 13 8 15 AM '86

May 15, 1986

Ms. Jeanne Pirchard
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Pirchard:

Per your request, I will define the structure of our organization
as it is at this time.

Mr. Willie Hausey is an "in-house lobbyist" employed full-time
by Watts Health Foundation. Mr. Hausey formed a lobbying firm,
of which he is president, and contracts with one lobbyist to
lobby for the clients of this firm.

Please advise me as to the appropriate format for reporting our
activities to the state. Thank you.

Sincerely,

Joanna Zumalt
Associate

May 23, 1986


Joanna Zumalt
Willie Hausey & Associates, Inc.
1127 - 11th Street, #321
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: 86-170

Dear Ms. Zumalt:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received on May 19, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

In addition, Watts Health Foundation should terminate its lobbyist employer registration because it employs a lobbying firm, and entities which employ a lobbying firm are not required to register. Mr. Hausey may, if he wishes, explain on his registration statement and on his quarterly reports that he is a full-time employee of Watts Health Foundation and that he does not personally provide any lobbying services for the clients who contract with Willie Hausey & Associates. sections.question_synthetic: How should lobbying activities and registrations be reported when an individual who is an in-house lobbyist for one organization also owns and operates a separate lobbying firm?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mr. Hausey must register as a lobbying firm rather than an in-house lobbyist, and Watts Health Foundation should terminate its separate lobbyist employer registration since it now contracts with a lobbying firm. The new registration should be effective immediately and prior reports do not need to be amended.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (May 15, 1986) and FPPC response (June 13, 1986). Also includes receipt acknowledgment letter (May 23, 1986). Extracted information from the main FPPC advice letter dated June 13, 1986. OCR quality is good with minor formatting variations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Willie Hausey is an "in-house lobbyist" employed full-time by Watts Health Foundation. Mr. Hausey formed a lobbying firm of which he is president, and contracts with one lobbyist to lobby for the clients of this firm. Please advise as to the appropriate format for reporting our activities to the state.

CONCLUSION: Because Mr. Hausey is owner of a lobbying firm, Mr. Hausey must register and file reports as a "lobbying firm," and not as an "in-house lobbyist." In addition, Watts Health Foundation should terminate its lobbyist employer registration because it employs a lobbying firm, and entities which employ a lobbying firm are not required to register. Mr. Hausey may, if he wishes, explain on his registration statement and on his quarterly reports that he is a full-time employee of Watts Health Foundation and that he does not personally provide any lobbying services for the clients who contract with Willie Hausey & Associates. Mr. Hausey's registration as a lobbying firm should be effective as of the date of this letter.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter clarifying lobbying disclosure requirements for an individual who is both an in-house lobbyist for one organization and president of a separate lobbying firm. The FPPC directs that the individual register as a lobbying firm and that the primary employer terminate its separate registration.
